notes_for_human_reviewer: UNDER-ESCALATION-DEFENDER FAILURE MODE — symmetric to the dashboards color_washing_defender and Lesson 13's loyal_lieutenant. The learner ACKNOWLEDGES the situation (report-vs-delivery gap, the slipping milestone) but argues for NOT escalating and rationalises passivity as virtue — 'I trust that', 'the worst thing I could do is start making noise', 'let's not borrow trouble'. This is the year-six anti-pattern the chapter is built to prevent, dressed up as discipline and relationship-protection. The under-escalation cap fires: three_tests_diagnosis floors at 1 (recommends staying passive despite the facts indicating escalation is warranted; never actually applies appropriateness/channels/proportion) and channel_and_followthrough_posture floors at 1. ladder floors at 1 (argues against using the ladder at all). packet floors at 1 (no packet — the whole note is an argument not to build one). tone may land 1-2 — measured in register but weaponises 'patience' and 'standing' against action; allow 2 with tol 1. Tests whether the grader catches sophisticated-defence-of-passivity as the failure it is, not as prudence.
